channel.title: Series on dasarpAI
channel.description: Recent content in Series on dasarpAI

item.title: The Complete Guide to Reconciliation Systems in BFSI
item.author: hari@dasarpai.com (Dr. Hari Thapliyaal)
item.description:
One financial event can appear in many systems. A card purchase may be recorded by a merchant, payment gateway, processor, card network, issuing bank, acquiring bank, and general ledger. Each system observes the event from a different position, applies its own timing and business rules, and stores its own version of the facts. Those records can disagree even when every system is working as designed.

Reconciliation is the disciplined process of comparing records from independent systems, explaining their differences, and establishing whether they represent the same underlying financial reality. It creates the evidence that allows institutions to trust balances, transactions, positions, obligations, and reported results.
